nodes.title: Remediate bdboard display-fidelity gaps (from the capability audit)
nodes.description: Umbrella for the prioritized follow-up work from the bdboard display-fidelity audit. Source: notes/analysis/bdboard-coverage/GAPS.md (synthesis bead bdboard-bk1r, audit epic bdboard-gnhi). These 14 children (FB-1..FB-14) are the ready-to-pour shortlist from 'Prioritized top-gaps shortlist -> recommended follow-up beads', ordered by severity then value. P3/P4 items were deliberately NOT filed (narrow-window, fall out of the FBs, deliberate boundaries, or disproven). Multi-in_progress / general-purpose use is the INTENDED design and is explicitly NOT a gap.
nodes.acceptance:
Success Criteria:
- The 2 P0/P1 readiness-misrepresentation fixes (FB-1, FB-3) land so no blocked/gate bead is shown as actionable READY work.
- The board's type/lane signal is legible (FB-2, FB-4) and coordination subsystems (gates/swarms) are no longer invisible (FB-5, FB-9, FB-10).
- Cheap already-held-data enrichments ship (FB-6 cycle badge, FB-7 in_progress KPI).
- Each child closes against its own acceptance criteria; none re-litigates multi-WIP as a defect.

nodes.title: Treat waits-for as a blocking edge in lane derivation (blocked-shown-as-ready)
nodes.description: _has_unmet_blocking_dep (lanes.py:126) and the epic-strip blocker check (lanes.py:236) gate only on ("blocks","blocked-by","blocked_by"). waits-for is one of bd's two blocking edge types (field guide ch2 2.4 / ch6 VI), so a bead gated solely by an unmet waits-for fanout edge is computed ready and rendered in the READY lane while bd excludes it from bd ready -- a blocked-shown-as-ready P0 (same class as bdboard-fjk). Add 'waits-for' to the blocker set, honoring vacuous-satisfy (a childless spawner means the waiter is genuinely ready). [FB-1, closes dep-graph#1 + gates#1, 2x P0 at one code site]
nodes.acceptance:
- a bead gated by an unmet waits-for edge lanes as Blocked, not Ready
- a waits-for bead whose spawner has no open children stays Ready (vacuous-satisfy)
- the _dep_label mapping is unaffected (no reversal regression)
- fixture test covers both the gated and vacuous cases

nodes.title: Render a per-type glyph/badge instead of flat grey issue_type text
nodes.description: All 9 bead types + event render as identical uppercase grey .bead-type text (bead_card.html:33, styles.css:800-805; modal scalar row). The field guide glyph system (task glyph, bug, feature, chore, epic, decision, spike, story, milestone) is wholly unreflected, so a milestone/gate/coordination bead is indistinguishable from a chore at a glance (memory bdboard-anatomy-type-flat). Map issue_type -> glyph + color on the card and in the modal header. [FB-2, root of gate (area 6) and mol-type (area 5) legibility gaps]
nodes.acceptance:
- each built-in type renders a distinct glyph + color on the card
- the modal header shows the type glyph
- an unknown/custom type falls back to the current grey text (no crash)

nodes.title: Special-case issue_type=gate so an open gate is shown as a wait, not ready work
nodes.description: gate beads flow through generic bucketing; an open ad-hoc gate has no unmet blocker of its own (its blocks edge points outward) so it lands in the READY lane as claimable work (lanes.py:314-345) -- the rubric's canonical P1. Also surface the await condition: await_type/await_id render only as raw alphabetical scalar rows (app.py:1580-1588) -- never a PR/run link, timer deadline, or manual-only bead-gate flag. Special-case issue_type=gate (own treatment/badge, framed as a pending wait) and parse await_type/await_id into a labeled gate-condition row. [FB-3]
nodes.acceptance:
- an open gate is not presented as claimable READY work
- gh:pr/gh:run await_id render as links; timer shows a deadline; bead gate flagged manual-only
- the gate->target blocks edge (already correct) is unchanged

nodes.title: Handle pinned/hooked statuses explicitly instead of dumping them in Deferred
nodes.description: pinned and hooked have no lane branch and fall through the else catch-all (lanes.py:341-342), so never-auto-close infra (pinned) and molecule/formula machinery (hooked) are shown as intentionally-parked Deferred work; the masthead also shows a pinned/hooked count with no matching lane. Add explicit handling (own lane or distinct card badge; consider hiding hooked like molecule wrappers). [FB-4, closes status#1 + status#2, same else hole]
nodes.acceptance:
- a pinned bead is visually distinct from a deferred bead
- a hooked bead is handled explicitly (badge or hidden), not shown as Deferred
- masthead counts and lanes agree (no count without a lane)

nodes.title: Split mol_type=swarm from formula-pour wrappers so running swarms are visible
nodes.description: The type-only _is_molecule filter (lanes.py:101-113,326) hides ALL molecule beads to suppress the redundant formula-pour grouping wrapper -- but it also hides mol_type=swarm molecules, the only object carrying a swarm's existence, coordinator/assignee, and bd swarm list rollup. A running swarm is invisible on the board (reachable only by direct modal URL). Detect mol_type=swarm and surface it (own strip/badge) while still hiding pour wrappers. [FB-5, do NOT un-hide pour wrappers (Option A stays)]
nodes.acceptance:
- a mol_type=swarm molecule earns a card/badge with its coordinator
- formula-pour grouping wrappers remain hidden (no regression to Option A)
- a non-swarm molecule wrapper is unchanged

nodes.title: Surface cycle / dangling-edge / incomplete-template badges in the lanes
nodes.description: bdboard surfaces zero graph-hygiene signals. (1) A dependency cycle is already detected in _topo_component_order (lanes.py:196) then silently discarded (:201) -- a deadlocked pair shows as ordinary Blocked; keep the result and badge affected beads (nearly free). (2) A structural orphan edge (blocks target absent from the snapshot) is masked as plain Blocked via the conservative target-is-None branch (lanes.py:130-132); distinguish blocked-by-missing from blocked-by-open. (3) No bd lint signal -- a bug missing ## Steps to Reproduce renders identically to a complete one; derive per-type required-section completeness and badge incomplete beads. [FB-6, coordinate with FB-1 on the lanes.py hot spot]
nodes.acceptance:
- beads in a cycle carry a cycle/deadlocked badge (reuse the existing detection)
- a bead blocked by a missing/unknown target is visually distinct from blocked-by-open
- incomplete-template beads show an indicator (per-type required sections)

nodes.title: Make in_progress a fixed masthead KPI and drop the single-flight framing
nodes.description: counts() docstring (lanes.py:390-399) and counts_skeleton.html assert 'bdboard is a single-flight workflow tool -- only one item is in-progress at a time' and omit the in_progress cell; real counts() re-adds it via the catch-all when >0, so the masthead grows a 5th cell on hydration (layout jitter) and the framing contradicts bdboard's general-purpose multi-WIP mission (proven: lanes render N>1 cleanly). Make in_progress a first-class fixed cell matching the skeleton; remove the single-flight prose. [FB-7, closes status#3 + swarms#7]
nodes.acceptance:
- in_progress is a fixed masthead cell present in both skeleton and live counts
- no layout jitter on hydration
- the single-flight docstring/comment is removed

nodes.title: Show Dolt sync state (ahead/behind/no-remote) and relabel the live pill
nodes.description: bdboard reads local Dolt and never calls bd dolt status/remote, so unpushed-local-writes (ahead of origin) and unpulled-remote-writes (behind origin) are invisible -- a teammate's bd dolt push leaves your board silently stale-vs-origin. The masthead 'live . push' pill (base.html:543) reflects only the SSE socket and is confusable with bd dolt push. Add a sync badge shelling bd dolt status / bd dolt remote list, and relabel the pill (e.g. 'live . updates'). [FB-8]
nodes.acceptance:
- a sync badge shows unpushed/behind/no-remote state
- shelling bd dolt status soft-fails (no 500) when no remote is configured
- the live pill no longer reads as a Dolt-sync indicator

nodes.title: Add a gates/coordination panel (bd gate list) and a merge-slot mutex affordance
nodes.description: bdboard never calls bd gate list/check or bd merge-slot check, so there is no gate overview, no pending-gate count, and merge-slots are indistinguishable from regular work (gt:slot only a modal chip; metadata.holder/waiters dumped as raw JSON, field_row.html:71). Add a gates/coordination panel shelling bd gate list --json (optional gate check / merge-slot check), and detect gt:slot to render an available/held-by-X + waiter-queue mutex affordance. [FB-9]
nodes.acceptance:
- a gates panel lists open gates with their await condition
- a merge-slot renders held/available + the waiter queue (not raw JSON)
- bd verb failures degrade gracefully (inline message, not 500)

nodes.title: Add an epic->children rollup backed by bd mol progress / bd swarm status + validate waves
nodes.description: Poured molecule children scatter across status lanes with no grouping/progress rollup back to the parent epic (formulas#2), and bd swarm's computed state -- progress %, Completed/Active/Ready/Blocked, coordinator, and the swarm validate WAVE model (parallel groups, max parallelism, worker-sessions) -- has no representation (swarms#2/#3). Add an epic-strip rollup (count/progress) backed by bd mol progress <id> --json, and a swarm view shelling bd swarm status/validate. [FB-10, merges formulas#2 + swarms#2/#3]
nodes.acceptance:
- the epic strip shows a child count/progress rollup
- a swarm surfaces progress % and Completed/Active/Ready/Blocked
- waves (Wave N, max parallelism) are legible for a swarmable epic

nodes.title: Capture pour stderr on success and warn when a vapor-phase formula is poured as persistent
nodes.description: pour_formula reads stderr only on non-zero exit (bd.py:825-827); bd mol pour prints the 'you poured a vapor-phase formula' warning on stderr with exit 0, so bdboard silently creates persistent, git-synced beads the formula author meant to be ephemeral (wisp). This is the one genuine write-surface misrepresentation in the pour flow. Capture pour stderr even on success and surface a 'this formula recommends wisp (ephemeral) -- poured as persistent' notice. [FB-11]
nodes.acceptance:
- a vapor-phase pour surfaces the recommend-wisp warning in formula_pour_result.html
- a normal (liquid) pour is unchanged
- the atomic pour result is never lost on a warning

nodes.title: Label the closed-window and surface sustained refresh-failure staleness
nodes.description: The Closed lane and CLOSED KPI only show closures within BOARD_CLOSED_WINDOW_DAYS=3 (lanes.py:40, bd.py:298-304) with no affordance, so a user cannot tell 'no old closures' from 'old closures hidden'. And a sustained bd list failure freezes the board on the previous snapshot (store.py serve-stale-on-failure) with only a log line -- no UI banner. Label the Closed lane with its window (+ a 'see History for older' link), and show a 'data stale -- last updated HH:MM' banner after N consecutive refresh failures. [FB-12, closes data-layer#2 + #3]
nodes.acceptance:
- the Closed lane states its active window and links to the uncapped History
- a sustained refresh failure surfaces a stale banner (not just a log line)
- a transient single failure does not flash the banner

nodes.title: Add a viewer for the swarm interaction log (.beads/interactions.jsonl)
nodes.description: The modal Audit trail/Lifecycle views are built entirely from bd history <id> (per-bead Dolt snapshots) and never read .beads/interactions.jsonl, so the cross-run llm_call/tool_call/label reward trail (the SFT/RL 'why did the agent do that' signal) is absent. Add an interaction-log viewer (separate from the per-bead history). [FB-13]
nodes.acceptance:
- the interaction log is readable in the UI (filter by kind: llm_call/tool_call/label)
- the existing per-bead bd history audit is unchanged
- a missing interactions.jsonl degrades gracefully

nodes.title: Render design as markdown + ordered, de-lossify the type dropdown, decode magic labels
nodes.description: Three anatomy field-render nits: (1) design is markdown-bearing (registry editor=md) and emitted by bd show --long, but absent from _FIELD_ORDER (sorts to the alphabetical tail) and _KIND_MARKDOWN (app.py:1362), so ADR rationale loses formatting -- add it to both. (2) The issue_type inline-edit dropdown _ISSUE_TYPE_OPTIONS (app.py:1441) lists only 6 of 9 types, so editing a spike/story/milestone cannot preserve its type -- source from bd types or add the missing built-ins. (3) Magic labels (dim:val, gt:slot, provides:X, export:X, template) render as undifferentiated chips -- style/decode them distinctly. [FB-14]
nodes.acceptance:
- design renders as markdown in the content group order
- the type dropdown can select every built-in type (non-lossy)
- magic labels are visually distinct from freeform tags
